title: Perovskite crystals can host qubits, challenging long-held assumptions
why:
- Researchers have demonstrated for the first time that perovskite materials can be used to create quantum bits (qubits).
- The technique allows for quick, affordable, and controllable creation of qubits, addressing the high cost and complexity of existing methods like those using diamonds.
- Perovskite-based qubits can potentially operate at higher temperatures than superconducting qubits, making the technology easier to scale.
- The qubits' optical signals can enable quantum communication using light in perovskite materials.
- This discovery challenges the prior belief that strong atomic interactions in perovskites would prevent qubit formation.
